briefdescription.para: Pan gesture detected signal type @SINCE_1_0.0.
Range of angles for a direction @SINCE_1_0.0.
For a left pan (-PI Radians).
For a right pan (0 Radians).
For an up pan (-0.5 * PI Radians).
For a down pan (0.5 * PI Radians).
For a left and right pan (PI Radians). Useful for AddDirection().
For an up and down pan (-0.5 * PI Radians). Useful for AddDirection().
The default threshold is PI * 0.25 radians (or 45 degrees).
Creates an uninitialized PanGestureDetector; this can be initialized with PanGestureDetector::New().

detaileddescription.para: Calling member functions with an uninitialized PanGestureDetector handle is not allowed. @SINCE_1_0.0

briefdescription.para: Destructor.

detaileddescription.para: This is non-virtual since derived Handle types must not contain data or virtual methods. @SINCE_1_0.0

briefdescription.para: This copy constructor is required for (smart) pointer semantics.
This assignment operator is required for (smart) pointer semantics.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
rhs
A reference to the copied handle
A reference to this

briefdescription.para: This move constructor is required for (smart) pointer semantics.
This move assignment operator is required for (smart) pointer semantics.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_2_2.4
rhs
A reference to the moved handle
A reference to this

briefdescription.para: This is the minimum number of touches required for the pan gesture to be detected.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
minimum
Minimum touches required
The gesture detector has been initialized.
The default minimum is '1'.

briefdescription.para: This is the maximum number of touches required for the pan gesture to be detected.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
maximum
Maximum touches required
The gesture detector has been initialized.
The default maximum is '1'.

briefdescription.para: This value is a maximum duration of motion can live on the pan gesture event queue. If duration exceed it, the motion event is discarded.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_2_1.28
maximumAge
Maximum age of motion events as milliseconds
The gesture detector has been initialized.
The default maximumAge is 'std::numeric_limits<uint32_t>::max()'.

briefdescription.para: Retrieves the minimum number of touches required for the pan gesture to be detected.
Retrieves the maximum number of touches required for the pan gesture to be detected.
Retrieves the maximum age for the pan gesture motion as milliseconds.
The pan gesture is only emitted if the pan occurs in the direction specified by this method with a +/- threshold allowance.

detaileddescription.para: The angle is from -180 -> 0 -> 180 degrees (or -M_PI -> 0 -> M_PI in radians) i.e:
If an angle of 0.0 degrees is specified and the threshold is 45 degrees then the acceptable direction range is from -45 to 45 degrees.

@SINCE_1_0.0
angle
The angle that pan should be allowed
threshold
The threshold around that angle
The gesture detector has been initialized.
The angle added using this API is only checked when the gesture first starts, after that, this detector will emit the gesture regardless of what angle the pan is moving.
The user can add as many angles as they require.
If an angle outside the range above is given, then it is wrapped within the range, i.e. 190 degrees becomes -170 degrees and 370 degrees becomes 10 degrees.
As long as you specify the type, you can also pass in a Dali::Degree to this method.
If no threshold is provided, then the default threshold (PI * 0.25) is used.
If the threshold is greater than PI, then PI will be used as the threshold.

briefdescription.para: A helper method for adding bi-directional angles where the pan should take place.

detaileddescription.para: In other words, if 0 is requested, then PI will also be added so that we have both left and right scrolling.

@SINCE_1_0.0
direction
The direction of panning required
threshold
The threshold
The gesture detector has been initialized.
If a direction outside the range above is given, then it is wrapped within the range, i.e. 190 degrees becomes -170 degrees and 370 degrees becomes 10 degrees.
If no threshold is provided, then the default threshold (PI * 0.25) is used.
If the threshold is greater than PI, then PI will be used as the threshold.
As long as you specify the type, you can also pass in a Dali::Degree to this method.
AddAngle

briefdescription.para: Returns the count of angles that this pan gesture detector emits a signal.
Returns the angle by index that this pan gesture detector emits a signal.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
index
The angle's index
An angle threshold pair, or a zero valued angle pair when index is invalid
The gesture detector has been initialized.
The index is less than GetAngleCount()

briefdescription.para: Clears any directional angles that are used by the gesture detector.

detaileddescription.para:
After this, the pan gesture will be emitted for a pan in ANY direction. @SINCE_1_0.0
The gesture detector has been initialized.

briefdescription.para: Removes the angle specified from the container.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
angle
The angle to remove
The gesture detector has been initialized.
This will only remove the first instance of the angle found from the container.
If an angle outside the range in AddAngle() is given, then the value is wrapped within the range and that is removed.

briefdescription.para: Removes the two angles that make up the direction from the container.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
direction
The direction to remove
The gesture detector has been initialized.
If a direction outside the range in AddAngle() is given, then the value is wrapped within the range and that is removed.

briefdescription.para: This signal is emitted when the pan gesture is detected on the attached actor.
Creates an initialized PanGestureDetector.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
A handle to a newly allocated Dali resource

briefdescription.para: Downcasts a handle to PanGestureDetector handle.

detaileddescription.para:
If handle points to a PanGestureDetector object, the downcast produces valid handle. If not, the returned handle is left uninitialized. @SINCE_1_0.0
handle
Handle to an object
Handle to a PanGestureDetector object or an uninitialized handle

briefdescription.para: Allows setting of the pan properties that are returned in constraints.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
pan
The pan gesture to set
If a normal pan is taking place, then any value set is ignored.

briefdescription.para: This class looks for panning (or dragging) gestures.

detaileddescription.para: The user will be pressing one or more fingers on an actor while they pan it.

@SINCE_1_0.0
PanGesture
Signals
Signal Name
Method
panDetected
DetectedSignal()
